project_type: Python AWS CDK app (Infrastructure as Code)
build_system: pip for dependencies, AWS CDK CLI for synthesis/deploy
test_framework: pytest with aws_cdk.assertions, coverage via pytest-cov, fixtures in tests/conftest.py
verification_instructions: 1. Run pytest tests/ - all tests must pass. 2. Run cdk synth - must succeed with no errors. 3. Run cdk diff - verify only expected resources appear.
setup_instructions: pip install -r requirements.txt -r requirements-dev.txt && npm install -g aws-cdk@2
environment_constraints: COMMON_DEPENDENCIES network - can install packages from allowlisted registries. Python 3.11 and Node 22 available. No Docker daemon needed for this project.
contribution_requirements: TDD-first: write failing tests before implementation. Conventional commit messages (feat:, fix:, chore:, docs:). Stack code in cdk_base/. Tests in tests/unit/. Update ARCHITECTURE.md after changes. Use L2 CDK constructs. Least-privilege IAM.
key_patterns: Stack defined in cdk_base/cdk_base_stack.py. Tests use a shared 'template' fixture from tests/conftest.py that synthesizes CdkBaseStack. Tests use aws_cdk.assertions (Template.has_resource_properties, resource_count_is, find_resources, Match helpers). State machine defined inline using sfn.DefinitionBody.from_chainable(). EventBridge targets state machine with InputPath $.detail.
directory_structure: cdk_base/ - CDK stack code. tests/unit/ - pytest unit tests using CDK assertions. tests/conftest.py - shared fixtures. ARCHITECTURE.md - system design doc (source of truth).
